20.summary: Graphics can now declare an optional thumbnail image in their manifest, giving controllers and editors a visual preview of each graphic.

25.summary: Manifests can now declare which rendering engine version they require, letting servers reject graphics that target an incompatible runtime.

27.summary: Added an optional property-order hint to manifest schemas so UIs can render fields in a predictable, designer-intended order.

46.summary: Designers can now mark which manifest fields contribute to a graphic's display name — making instances easier to identify in playlists.

63.summary: The OGraf specification is officially licensed under MIT — clearing up any ambiguity about commercial use, forks, and derivative works.

50.summary: Manifests can now group related schema properties together, so form UIs can render them under labelled sections instead of a flat list.

47.summary: Added position metadata to GDD types so layout-sensitive graphics (lower thirds, bugs, tickers) carry their intended screen placement.

54.summary: Graphics that need internet access (live data, remote fonts, external APIs) now declare it up front — letting operators run them in air-gapped environments safely.

42.summary: Removed the frame-count return from goToTime(). Graphics just seek to the time and return — less ceremony, fewer edge cases.

49.summary: GDD gained a masked-text input type for sensitive fields like passwords, API keys, or anything that shouldn't show in plain text in the controller UI.

24.summary: Graphics Data Definition (GDD), previously a separate spec, was merged into OGraf itself — one spec, one source of truth, one place to look things up.

41.summary: GDD added a drop-down input type so manifests can constrain fields to a fixed set of choices without custom validation logic.

35.summary: Clarified exactly when playAction() should resolve its promise — removing ambiguity between 'started playing' and 'finished playing'.

29.summary: Tightened the spec around how OGraf graphics expose themselves as Web Components — making implementations more consistent across renderers.

22.summary: Documented the implications of AdditionalProperties: false in schemas — it's strict by design, but it bites if you're not expecting it during validation.

36.summary: Clarified the distinction between step-based graphics (discrete stages) and updateAction-driven graphics (continuous data updates). Two patterns, two use cases.

18.summary: Manifest files now use the *.ograf.json extension convention — making OGraf graphics instantly recognisable in a filesystem or repo.

9.summary: Discussed how graphics should render preview images for controllers and editors — groundwork for the later Thumbnail decision (#20).

1.summary: Set the direction for how OGraf graphics should load and reference fonts — recognising fonts as a first-class concern for broadcast-quality typography.

5.summary: Concluded GDD is still needed — paving the way for its eventual merge into OGraf itself (see #24).

2.summary: Fixed the sample manifest files to correctly reference their JavaScript implementations — otherwise the examples wouldn't actually run.
